author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Women-s Ordination Retreat - Questions and Answers February 1988
content:
QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS WITH SANGHARAKSHITA


Ordination Retreat for Kulasri and Kulaprabha, February 1988

Contents

1 Is competition necessarily related to the power mode and antithetical to the
Bodhichitta
4 Why did the Arahants not cry when the Buddha died?
5 An Enlightened being does not experience sadness
6 More elaborate ritual in the FWBO
7 Wanting to belong to a group or something
8 Acknowledgement of people's requests to become Mitras
9 Mahastamaprapta
10 The Pure Land and the Trikaya doctrine
11 Ways of practising the fifth stage of the Mettā Bhāvana
Having a good sense of direction (in the normal 'mundane', practical sense)
13 The five niyamas and evolving consciousness
15 Insight arising as a response to beauty, happiness or pleasure?
18 Difficulties for men in Western cultures to develop feminine qualities
21 Rules and criteria/principles in the spiritual community
22 Emphasis in the FWBO on visualising Bodhisattvas as a vipassana practice
23 Practising counselling within the FWBO. Kalyana Mitrata and counselling
26 Where should a shrine room activity leader sit in relation to the Buddha?
27 A 'personal myth'
30 Psychological types and self understanding
Astrology and the Dharma
31 Vajrapani
32 The blue throated Avalokitesvara
